field_description: Classifies the general runtime value shape of a field, such as string, integer, boolean, array, object, binary, or null. Used by validation, form generation, import/export, and database mapping.

Lists validation constraints that determine whether a text value is acceptable. This field may be stored as an array when multiple constraints apply. Cleanup or normalization behavior belongs in type_input_cleanup instead.

field_option.option_description: Allows letters, numbers, and dashes only.
Allows letters, numbers, and underscores only.
Allows letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores only.
Requires at least one special character.
Requires a valid IP address format.
Requires a valid IPv4 address format.
Requires a valid IPv6 address format.

field_description: Reusable field template for Display Layout Mode values.

field_option.option_description: Select Inherited for Display Layout Mode.
Select List for Display Layout Mode.
Select Tabular for Display Layout Mode.
Select Detail for Display Layout Mode.
Select Content for Display Layout Mode.
Select Thumbnail for Display Layout Mode.
Select Picture for Display Layout Mode.
Select Collage for Display Layout Mode.
Select Video for Display Layout Mode.
Select Calendar for Display Layout Mode.
Select Graph for Display Layout Mode.

field_description: Reusable field template for CRUD Mode values.
Reusable field template for Operation Type values.

field_option.option_description: Select Retrieve / View for Operation Type.
Select Update / Edit for Operation Type.

field_description: Classifies the stored representation format of a text field, especially for text areas, rich-text editors, code editors, import/export fields, and generated validation.

Classifies cleanup or normalization operations that may be applied before saving a field value. These options alter input; they are separate from validation constraints that only accept or reject input.

field_option.option_description: Remove leading and trailing spaces before saving.
Collapse repeated internal spaces according to the cleanup implementation.
Convert text to uppercase before saving.
Convert text to lowercase before saving.
Convert text to title case only when language-aware title-casing rules are available or the user explicitly confirms the preview.
Normalize version-like input to the approved version-number format.
Normalize Unicode text using NFC normalization.
Normalize Unicode text using NFD normalization.

field_description: Classifies the user-interface control or display component normally used for a field in forms, generated screens, or schema-aware editors.

field_option.option_description: Clickable element that performs an action when activated.
Calendar control for selecting or viewing dates.
Checkbox control for boolean or option selection.
Drop-down list combined with editable text input.
Control for selecting date and time together.
Control for selecting a date from a calendar.
Drop-down list for selecting one option.
Control for selecting or uploading a file.
Table-like control for rows and columns.
Hidden field not visible to the user.
Image selection, upload, or display control.
List box control for selectable items.
Link control or link display field.
Input control for passwords or secret text.
Control for selecting a referenced record.
Slider control for selecting a value within a range.
Control for selecting or entering tags.
Input control for entering time values.
Control for selecting a time value.
Editor for source code or structured code-like text.

field_description: Classifies an optional SQL storage type suggestion used by SQL generators such as MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL Server, and other relational targets. It does not replace type_data.

field_option.option_description: SQL set type for multiple allowed values.
UUID SQL type or UUID storage suggestion.

field_description: Classifies advanced validation rules used for conditional, cross-field, cross-collection, regular-expression, API, or custom validation. Ordinary required, length, number, option, and text-constraint rules should remain top-level field properties.

field_option.option_description: Requires this value to be after another field value.
Requires this value to be before another field value.
Requires this value to equal another field value.
Requires this value to differ from another field value.
Requires this value to be greater than another field value.
Requires this value to be less than another field value.

field_option.option_label: Greater Than or Equal to Field
field_option.option_description: Requires this value to be greater than or equal to another field value.

field_option.option_label: Less Than or Equal to Field
field_option.option_description: Requires this value to be less than or equal to another field value.
Requires this field only when a condition is met.
Validates this value using a regular expression.
Requires this value to be unique within a collection or configured scope.
Requires this value to exist in another collection or lookup source.
Requires this value not to exist in another collection or lookup source.
Validates this value through an API or external check.
Uses a custom validation rule defined by the project or generator profile.

field_description: Classifies comparison operators used in filter rules, reference picker filters, imports, search screens, and generated query helpers.

field_option.option_description: Matches values equal to the filter value.
Matches values not equal to the filter value.
Matches values greater than the filter value.
Matches values greater than or equal to the filter value.
Matches values less than the filter value.
Matches values less than or equal to the filter value.
Matches values containing the filter value.
Matches values not containing the filter value.
Matches values contained in a list.
Matches values not contained in a list.
Matches values between two boundary values.
Matches text values that start with the filter value.
Matches text values that end with the filter value.
Matches values using a regular expression.

field_description: Reusable field template for Form Type values.

field_option.option_description: Form used to create or enter a new record.
Form used to update an existing record.
Form used to create a new record from an existing record.
Form used to split one record or value into multiple records or values.
Form used to combine one record with another record.
Form used to create multiple records or fields at once.
Form used to update multiple records or fields at once.
Form used to search for records or fields.
Form used to narrow records by selected criteria.
Form used to compare records, fields, or values.
Form used to match records or values against possible candidates.
Form used to reconcile conflicting or unmatched data.
Form used to import data from an external source.
Form used to export data to another format or destination.
Form used to upload files or submitted content.
Form used to move or transform data during migration.
Form used to map fields, columns, or values between structures.
Form used to convert data from one shape, unit, or format to another.
Form used to calculate values from entered or selected data.
Form used to enter, review, or manage translations.
Form used to configure or run generated output.
Form used to register a user, account, or entity.
Form used to sign in to an account or secured area.
Form used to view or manage profile information.
Form used to manage settings or configuration values.
Form used to plan dates, times, or recurring events.
Form used to reserve a time, place, service, or resource.
Form used to collect answers, observations, or feedback.
Form used to submit a request for action, approval, or service.
Form used to invite a person or entity to participate.
Form used to guide a user through a multi-step process.
Form used to capture or confirm a signature.
Form used to verify a one-time password or code.
